patch_description:
Propose a fundamentally different ansatz design by dramatically reusing parameters across gates to reduce model complexity and improve generalization. The new ANSATZ_SPEC uses:
- shared pre/post RZ layers grouped by board symmetry (corner/edge/center)
- a ring entangler using CRX gates with a single shared parameter around the outer perimeter (crx_ring)
- hub entanglement using CRY gates from center to each edge, all sharing a single parameter (cry_hub_edge)
This drastically reduces per-block parameter count (shared across eight ring gates and four hub gates), while preserving data-uploading reuploading structure and the fixed training loop. The architecture remains within the adjacency constraints and continues to output per-qubit Z expectations mapped to [cross, circle, draw].
